chapter: General Principles and Processes of Isolation of Metals
solution: Reduction of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>Al</mi><mn>2</mn></msub><msub><mi mathvariant="normal">O</mi><mn>3</mn></msub><mo>→</mo><mi>Al</mi></math> is carried out by electrolytic reduction of its fused salts. <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>ZnO</mi><mo>,</mo><mo> </mo><msub><mi>Fe</mi><mn>2</mn></msub><msub><mi mathvariant="normal">O</mi><mn>3</mn></msub></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>Cu</mi><mn>2</mn></msub><mi mathvariant="normal">O</mi></math> can be reduced by carbon.

subject: Chemistry
chapter: General Principles and Processes of Isolation of Metals
solution:
The sulphide ore of iron is iron pyrite.

Iron can be extracted from iron pyrites

Galena is the sulphide ore of lead (PbS)

The sulphide containing ore of zinc is zinc blende (ZnS).

Al is extracted from Al2O3·2H2O i.e., Bauxite ore

Hence, Iron, lead and zinc can be extracted from their sulphide ores but not aluminium.

subject: Chemistry
chapter: General Principles and Processes of Isolation of Metals
solution:
Calamine, Siderite and Malachite are carbonate containing ores. Sphalerite is a sulphide ore.

Calamine: ZnCO3

Siderite: FeCO3

Sphalerite : ZnS

Malachite : CuCO3·CuOH2

subject: Chemistry
chapter: General Principles and Processes of Isolation of Metals
solution:
Ellingham diagram is a graphical representation of ΔG vs T when metal heated with oxygen to form metal oxide.

An Ellingham diagram is a graph showing the temperature dependence of the stability for compounds. This analysis is usually used to evaluate the ease of reduction of metal oxides and sulphides.

Ellingham diagram represents change in free energy ΔG with temperature

options: roasting followed by reduction with carbon
roasting followed by reduction with another metal

subject: Chemistry
chapter: General Principles and Processes of Isolation of Metals
options: Both A and R are true but R is NOT the correct explanation of A
A is false but R is true
Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
A is true but R is false
solution:
For line II ∆S is more negative than line I. In the Ellingham diagram, a sharp change in slope of the line is observed for Mg-MgO at ~1120oC because that is the boiling point of magnesium.

From diagram, ∆SI=(Ssolid)-(Ssolid+Sgas)∆SII=(Sliquid)-(Ssolid+Sgas)

There is a large increase in entropy associated with the change of state of magnesium.

So, both Assertion (A) and Reason (R) are true and (R) is the correct explanation of (A).

subject: Chemistry
chapter: General Principles and Processes of Isolation of Metals
solution:
Siderite: FeCO3

Malachite: CuCO3.CuOH2

Magnetite: Fe3O4

Haematite: Fe2O3

Cryolite: Na3AlF6

Cuprite: Cu2O

Limonite: FeOOH.nH2O

Kaolinite: Al2OH4Si2O5

Sphalerite: ZnS

Bauxite: AlOxOH3-2x

So, as we can see that among the given options 4 ores (Siderite, magnetite, Haematite, Limonite) contain iron.

subject: Chemistry
chapter: General Principles and Processes of Isolation of Metals
options: the conditions of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>pH</mi></math> and potential under which a species is thermodynamically stable.
the temperature dependence of the standard Gibbs energies of formation of some metal oxides.
the pressure dependence of the standard electrode potentials of reduction reactions involved in the extraction of metals.
the kinetics of the reduction process.
solution:
Ellingham diagram is graph of ΔG° vs T of any metal/element oxide. since

ΔG°=ΔH°-TΔS°

for most metal oxide formation metal (s) + oxygen (g) →metal oxide (s)

ΔH°=-ve

ΔS°=-ve

so graph will be a straight line with - ve y-intracept & + ve slope.

options: At <math><msup><mrow><mn>1400</mn></mrow><mrow><mi>o</mi></mrow></msup><mi>C</mi><mo>,</mo><mi>A</mi><mi>l</mi></math> can be used for the extraction of <math><mi>Z</mi><mi>n</mi><mi> </mi></math> from <math><mi>Z</mi><mi>n</mi><mi>O</mi></math> .

At <math><msup><mrow><mn>500</mn></mrow><mrow><mi>o</mi></mrow></msup><mi>C</mi><mo>,</mo></math> coke can be used for the extraction of <math><mi>Z</mi><mi>n</mi><mi> </mi></math> from  <math><mi>Z</mi><mi>n</mi><mi>O</mi></math>

At <math><msup><mrow><mn>800</mn></mrow><mrow><mi>o</mi></mrow></msup><mi>C</mi><mo>,</mo><mi>C</mi><mi>u</mi></math> can be used for the extraction of <math><mi>Z</mi><mi>n</mi><mi> </mi></math> from  <math><mi>Z</mi><mi>n</mi><mi>O</mi></math>
Coke cannot be used for the extraction of <math><mi>C</mi><mi>u</mi><mi> </mi></math> from  <math><mi>C</mi><msub><mrow><mi>u</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>2</mn></mrow></msub><mi>O</mi></math>
solution:
According to the Ellingham diagram, the species that is going to act as reducing agent should lie below the substance to be reduced at the particular temperature.

 As is visible from the graph, at 1400o C, the line of Al lies below the line of ZnO. Hence, Al can be used for reducing ZnO to Zn.

solution:
In actual practice, the ore is heated in reverberatory furnace after mixing with silica. In the furnace, iron oxide 'slags off' as iron silicate and copper is produced in the form copper matte. This contains Cu2S and FeS.

FeO+SiO2⟶FeSiO3

subject: Chemistry
chapter: General Principles and Processes of Isolation of Metals
solution: Froth flotation is a process for selectively separating hydrophobic materials from hydrophilic. The principle of froth flotation process is that sulphide ores are preferentially wetted by the pine oil, whereas the gangue particles are wetted by the water. Cresols and aniline are used as froth stabilisers.

chapter: General Principles and Processes of Isolation of Metals
options: Both Statement I and Statement II are correct.
Both Statement I and Statement II are not correct.
Statement I is correct but Statement II is not correct
Statement I is not correct but Statement II is correct.
solution:
Statement-I is incorrect because cast iron is obtained by heating pig iron with scrap iron. Statement-II is also incorrect because pig iron has more carbon content ~4% Than cast iron ~3%.

So, Both Statement I and Statement II are not correct.

subject: Chemistry
chapter: General Principles and Processes of Isolation of Metals
options: <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">A</mi><mo>,</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">B</mi><mo>,</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">D</mi></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">E</mi></math> only
solution:
Electrolytic refining is a technique that is used for the extraction and purification of metals that are obtained by refining methods. The impure metal is used as an anode and the pure metal is used a cathode. Soluble salt from the same metal is used an electrolyte.

Liquation process is used for the purification of the metal, which itself is readily fusible, but the impurities present in it are not, used for the purification of Sn and Zn, and for removing Pb from Zn-Aq alloy.

Leaching is a process widely used in extractive metallurgy where ore is treated with chemicals to convert the valuable metals within into soluble salts while the impurity remains insoluble.

Calcination is the process in which the ore of the metal is heated to high temperature in the absence or limited supply of air or oxygen.

Distillation: This method is used for the purification of metals which possess a low boiling point such as mercury and zinc.

subject: Chemistry
chapter: General Principles and Processes of Isolation of Metals
solution:
The following changes occur during smelting.

(a) Most of the ferrous sulphide gets oxidised to ferrous oxide which combines with silica (flux) to form fusible slag.

2FeS + 3O2 → 2FeO + 2SO2

FeO + SiO2 → FeSiO3 (Ferrous silicate)

The slag being lighter floats and forms the upper layer. It is removed through the slag hole from time to time.

(b) During roasting or in the blast furnace if any oxide of copper is formed, it combines with FeS and is changed back into its sulphide.

2Cu2S + 3O2 → 2Cu2O + 2SO2

Cu2O + FeS → Cu2S + FeO

Ferrous oxide thus formed again combines with silica to form more slag.

FeO+SiO2→FeSiO3

So, no formation of calcium silicate CaSiO3 in extraction of Cu.

solution:
Siderite $=\mathrm{FeCO}_{3}$

Calamine $=\mathrm{ZnCO}_{3}$

Malachite $=\mathrm{CuCO}_{3} \cdot \mathrm{Cu}(\mathrm{OH})_{2}$

Kaolinite $=\mathrm{Al}_{2} \mathrm{Si}_{2} \mathrm{O}_{5}(\mathrm{OH})_{4}$

Copper glance : Cu2S; Cuprite : Cu2O 

Malachite : Cu(OH)2.CuCO3 ; Chalcopyrite : CuFeS2

 Malachite is a copper carbonate hydroxide mineral with the formula Cu(OH)2.CuCO3.

<math><mo>(</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">I</mi><mo>)</mo><mo>-</mo><mo>(</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">b</mi><mo>)</mo><mo>;</mo><mi mathvariant="normal"> </mi><mo>(</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">I</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">I</mi><mo>)</mo><mo>-</mo><mo>(</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">d</mi><mo>)</mo><mo>;</mo><mi mathvariant="normal"> </mi><mo>(</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">I</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">I</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">I</mi><mo>)</mo><mo>-</mo><mo>(</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">a</mi><mo>)</mo><mo>;</mo><mi mathvariant="normal"> </mi><mo>(</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">I</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">V</mi><mo>)</mo><mo>-</mo><mo>(</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">c</mi><mo>)</mo></math>

<math><mfenced separators="|"><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">I</mi></mrow></mfenced><mo>-</mo><mfenced separators="|"><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">c</mi></mrow></mfenced><mo>;</mo><mi mathvariant="normal"> </mi><mfenced separators="|"><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">I</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">I</mi></mrow></mfenced><mo>-</mo><mfenced separators="|"><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">d</mi></mrow></mfenced><mo>;</mo><mi mathvariant="normal"> </mi><mfenced separators="|"><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">I</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">I</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">I</mi></mrow></mfenced><mo>-</mo><mfenced separators="|"><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">b</mi></mrow></mfenced><mo>;</mo><mi mathvariant="normal"> </mi><mfenced separators="|"><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">I</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">V</mi></mrow></mfenced><mo>-</mo><mfenced separators="|"><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">a</mi></mrow></mfenced></math>

<math><mfenced separators="|"><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">I</mi></mrow></mfenced><mo>-</mo><mo>(</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">c</mi><mo>)</mo><mo>;</mo><mi mathvariant="normal"> </mi><mfenced separators="|"><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">I</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">I</mi></mrow></mfenced><mo>-</mo><mfenced separators="|"><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">a</mi></mrow></mfenced><mo>;</mo><mi mathvariant="normal"> </mi><mo>(</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">I</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">I</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">I</mi><mo>)</mo><mo>-</mo><mfenced separators="|"><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">b</mi></mrow></mfenced><mo>;</mo><mi mathvariant="normal"> </mi><mfenced separators="|"><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">I</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">V</mi></mrow></mfenced><mo>-</mo><mo>(</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">d</mi><mo>)</mo></math>
solution:
Liquation is used to purify the metal whose melting point is lower than that of impurities like Sn, Bi,Pb etc.
Mond’s process The impure nickle is heated in presence of carbon monoxide. 

Nis + COg →NiCO4g → Nis + 4COg
Zone refining is based on the principle that impurities are more soluble in melt that in the pure solid metal. The metals like Ga, Ge etc. are purified by this method.
Van Arkell method  is used for those metals which are very difficult to obtain in pure state like Zr.

chapter: General Principles and Processes of Isolation of Metals
solution: Froth stabilizers cresols, aniline are added to stabilize the froth and enhance the nonwettability of the mineral particles.

subject: Chemistry
chapter: General Principles and Processes of Isolation of Metals
solution: Zinc can be purified economically by fractional distillation because of low melting point.

subject: Chemistry
chapter: General Principles and Processes of Isolation of Metals
solution:
Calcination is a process in which ore is heated in the absence of air or air might be supplied in a limited quantity. 

Roasting involves the heating of ore lower than its melting point in the presence of air or oxygen.

2PbS+3O2→∆2PbO+2SO2

The above reaction is an example of roasting. Rest all the reactions are heated in the absence of oxygen.

subject: Chemistry
chapter: General Principles and Processes of Isolation of Metals
solution:
For ZnS,KCN is used as depressant.

In the metallurgy of silver and gold, the respective metal is leached with a dilute solution of NaCN or KCN in the presence of air, which supplies O2. The metal is obtained later by replacement reaction.

4Ms+8CN-aq+2H2Oaq+O2g→4MCN2-aq+4OH-aq M=Ag or Au

2MCN2-aq+Zns→ZnCN42-aq+2Ms

Copper metal is not involved in this process.

Metal sulphides are less stable than the corresponding oxides
solution: No Solution Available

subject: Chemistry
chapter: General Principles and Processes of Isolation of Metals
solution:
The solution of dicyano complexes of gold (Au) and silver (Ag) are used as electrolytes while performing electroplating with them.

The amine complexes of gold are not stable, so, they cannot be used.

Silver cyanide is employed as cyanide reacts with silver to form stable complexes.

subject: Chemistry
chapter: General Principles and Processes of Isolation of Metals
solution: Reduction by powdered aluminium is known as Gold-Schmidt aluminothermic process. This process is employed in cases where metals have very high m.p. and are to be extracted from their oxides.

subject: Chemistry
chapter: General Principles and Processes of Isolation of Metals
solution:
Hall Heroult's process:

At cathode: Al3++3e⊝→Al

At anode: O2-+C→CO+2e⊝

Overall reaction: 2Al2O3+3C→4Al+3CO2

chapter: General Principles and Processes of Isolation of Metals
solution:
(a) Mercury → Distillation refining
(b) Copper → Electrolytic refining
(c) Silicon → Zone refining
(d) Nickel → Vapour phase refining

options: converts copper sulphide into copper silicate
converts iron oxide into iron silicate
reduces copper sulphide into metallic copper
reduces the melting point of the reaction mixture
solution: Silica is used to remove FeO impurity from the ore of copper

subject: Chemistry
chapter: General Principles and Processes of Isolation of Metals
solution:
Reverberatory furnace, in copper, tin, and nickel production, a furnace used for smelting or refining in which the fuel is not in direct contact with the ore but heats it by a flame blown over it from another chamber.Purification of aluminium by electrolytic refining is known as Hoope's process. By this process 99.9% pure aluminium metal is obtained.

The iron manufactured in Blast Furnaces contain about 3 – 4 % of Carbon and smaller quantities of many other impurities such as sulphur, Silicon, etc. This is called Pig Iron.

Zone refining is used for the purification of metals like Si, Ge, Ga which are used in semiconductors.

subject: Chemistry
chapter: General Principles and Processes of Isolation of Metals
solution: Silver ore forms a soluble complex with NaCN from which silver is precipitated using scrap zinc.

subject: Chemistry
chapter: General Principles and Processes of Isolation of Metals
